ddls:
CREATE TABLE "artists" (
  "artist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each artist */,
  "artist_name" TEXT /* Name of the artist */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biographical information about the artist */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL to the artist's image */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the artist */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the artist */,
  "social_media_links" TEXT /* Links to the artist's social media profiles */,
  "label_id" INTEGER /* ID of the record label associated with the artist */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("artist_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "albums" (
  "album_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each album */,
  "artist_id" INTEGER /* ID of the artist who released the album */,
  "album_title" TEXT /* Title of the album */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Year the album was released */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the album */,
  "total_certified_copies" TEXT /* Total certified copies sold (from available markets) */,
  "claimed_sales" INTEGER /* Claimed sales of the album */,
  "ref_sources" TEXT /* References and sources for the sales data */,
  "producer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the producer of the album */,
  "album_type" TEXT /* Type of the album (e.g., studio, live, compilation) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Exact release date of the album */,
  "is_certified" INTEGER /* Indicates if the album has been certified */,
  "certification_level" TEXT /* Certification level of the album (e.g., Gold, Platinum) */,
  "certification_date" TEXT /* Date of certification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("album_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_albums_artist_id FOREIGN KEY ("artist_id") REFERENCES artists ("artist_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_albums_genre FOREIGN KEY ("genre") REFERENCES genres ("genre_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_by_country" (
  "sales_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales record */,
  "album_id" INTEGER /* ID of the album */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* Country code (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2) */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "sales_volume" INTEGER /* Sales volume in the country */,
  "sales_date" TEXT /* Date of the sales data */,
  "is_projected" INTEGER /* Indicates if the sales volume is a projection */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the sales volume */,
  "exchange_rate" REAL /* Exchange rate for converting sales volume to a standard currency */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sales_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_by_country_album_id FOREIGN KEY ("album_id") REFERENCES albums ("album_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  "parent_genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent genre */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the genre is currently active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genres_parent_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, artist, label) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the album data */,
  "album_id" INTEGER /* ID of the album being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the album data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user's browser */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_album_id FOREIGN KEY ("album_id") REFERENCES albums ("album_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
